methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Apis Cor is a seed-stage company operating in Housing & Construction, a sector with limited public comparables (zero) and sparse private precedent (three identified peers: ICON, BotBuilt, and American Housing Corporation). The company has raised $5.5M to date.

Valuation was conducted using stage-progression methodology, the sole applicable triangulation approach. Peer-median analysis could not be applied; the Housing & Construction sector yielded only one same-sector comparable, insufficient to meet the minimum threshold of three datapoints required for a reliable median. A stage-progression model using the $6M total-raised proxy as a base and applying a 2.5x multiplier—typical for progression from Seed to Series A—implies a valuation of $14M.

The analysis supports a range of $12M to $16M, centered at $14M. The primary uncertainty stems from the absence of tight peer benchmarks in this sector, limiting cross-sectional validation. A second material uncertainty is the appropriate stage-progression multiplier itself; consumer-facing or capital-intensive construction tech may exhibit different step-up patterns than venture-backed software. Any refinement to this valuation should prioritize accumulation of comparable transaction data within specialized construction-tech verticals.
